How Much Does Tree Removal Cost in Concord NC?

May 15, 2026 | 6 min read

Tree removal cost in Concord NC ranges from $250 to $2,500 for most residential jobs. The final number depends on tree size, location on your property, and how complex the job is to execute safely.

Tree Removal Prices in Concord by Size

Tree height is the primary factor driving your cost. A shorter tree takes less time, fewer cuts, and lighter equipment. Taller trees require rigging, sectional removal, and often a dedicated crane or bucket truck.

Here is what most Concord homeowners pay based on tree height:

  • Under 25 feet (ornamental trees, small pines): $150 to $500
  • 25 to 50 feet (average yard trees, dogwoods, smaller oaks): $500 to $1,000
  • 50 to 75 feet (mature oaks, large pines, sweetgums): $1,000 to $1,800
  • Over 75 feet (very large hardwoods, old growth): $1,500 to $3,000+

Concord sits in the Piedmont region where loblolly pines, sweetgums, and red oaks are common. Most residential removals in Cabarrus County fall in the $400 to $1,200 range because the average yard tree here runs 30 to 60 feet tall.

What Else Affects the Price

Height is not the only factor. Several conditions can push the cost higher or bring it down.

Proximity to structures. A tree leaning over your roof, near a fence, or tangled in power lines requires more cautious rigging. Crews may need to remove the tree in small sections instead of felling it in one direction. Expect a 20 to 40 percent increase in price for trees near homes or utility lines.

Trunk diameter. Two 40-foot trees can have very different trunk sizes. A 30-inch diameter trunk takes significantly more cutting time and produces more wood to haul than a 12-inch trunk.

Access to the tree. If your crew can drive equipment right up to the tree, the job goes faster. If the tree is in a fenced backyard, behind a shed, or on a steep slope, the crew has to carry everything by hand. Limited access adds time and cost.

Number of trees. Most companies offer a discount when you remove multiple trees at once. The crew and equipment are already on-site, so the per-tree cost drops. If you have three or four trees to take down, ask about a package price.

Deadwood or storm damage. Dead trees are unpredictable. Branches can snap during removal, and the trunk may be hollow or structurally compromised. Emergency tree removal after storms can cost more due to urgency and hazardous conditions, with an average around $843 in the Concord area.

Every property is different. The fastest way to get an accurate price is a free on-site estimate.

Call (704) 313-1555 for a free tree removal estimate

Stump Grinding: An Extra Cost to Plan For

Tree removal usually does not include the stump. Most companies charge separately for stump grinding, which typically runs $100 to $400 in Concord depending on the stump diameter and root spread.

Grinding takes the stump down 6 to 12 inches below grade. The hole gets filled with the wood chips, and within a season your lawn grows over the spot. If you skip grinding, the stump attracts termites and carpenter ants, and new shoots will sprout from the roots for years.

Ask your tree service if they offer a bundled price for removal plus grinding. Most will knock $50 to $100 off the stump grinding cost when done alongside the removal.

Does Tree Trimming Cost Less Than Removal?

Yes, significantly. Tree trimming and pruning in Concord runs $150 to $1,200 depending on tree size and how much work is needed. Trimming preserves the tree while removing problem branches.

If your tree is healthy but has branches scraping your roof, hanging over a driveway, or blocking sunlight, trimming is the more affordable option. Not sure which service you need? Read our guide: What's the Difference Between Tree Trimming and Tree Removal?

How to Save on Tree Removal in Concord

There are a few ways to get the most value:

  • Get three quotes. Prices vary widely between companies. Getting multiple estimates ensures you are paying a fair rate. Here is what to ask each company.
  • Schedule in late fall or winter. The dormant season (November through February) is slower for tree companies. Many offer lower rates during this period.
  • Bundle multiple trees. Removing two or three trees at once is almost always cheaper per tree than separate visits.
  • Keep the wood. If you have a fireplace or fire pit, ask the crew to leave the firewood-length logs. Hauling is a cost for the company, so offering to keep the wood can shave a bit off the price.
  • Check your homeowner's insurance. If a tree fell due to a storm or is threatening your home, your insurance may cover part or all of the removal cost. Call your adjuster before you call the tree service.

Do You Need a Permit in Concord?

Concord does not require permits for removing trees on private residential property in most cases. However, if your property falls within a historic district or has specific HOA covenants, you may need approval before removing certain trees. If your tree is near a property line, talk to your neighbor first to avoid disputes.

When trees are near power lines, do not attempt removal yourself. Call your tree service and they will coordinate with Duke Energy if needed.

Get Your Free Estimate

Every tree is different, and online estimates can only get you so far. The best way to know your exact cost is to have a crew come look at the tree in person. We provide free, no-obligation estimates for homeowners across Concord, Kannapolis, Harrisburg, and the surrounding Midland and Mount Pleasant areas.

Request your free estimate online or call (704) 313-1555 to schedule.

Tree Removal Cost FAQs

What is the average tree removal cost in Concord NC?

Most homeowners pay between $400 and $1,200 for a standard residential tree removal. The average across the Concord area is around $564. Smaller trees under 25 feet cost as little as $150, while large hardwoods over 75 feet can run $2,500 or more.

Does the price include stump removal?

Usually not. Stump grinding is a separate service that costs $100 to $400 in Concord. Ask for a bundled quote when scheduling your tree removal to save on the combined cost.

Is emergency tree removal more expensive?

Yes. Emergency tree removal averages around $843 in Concord due to urgency, after-hours response, and hazardous conditions from storm damage.

When is the cheapest time to remove a tree?

Late fall through winter (November to February) is typically the slowest season for tree services. Many companies offer lower rates during this period because demand is lower.
